header.headline: Engineering Compass
header.subtitle: Profile, measure, fix, repeat: follow the bottleneck rather than the hype cycle.

left.title: Hardware spectrum
left.body: Different systems expose different ceilings, but the method stays the same: measure before you mythologize.

right.title: Loop discipline
right.body: One bottleneck at a time keeps the program legible and avoids speculative architectural churn.

panels.title: Profile first
panels.summary: Instrumentation comes before optimization because intuition is usually wrong about the active bottleneck.

panels.title: Fix one gate
panels.summary: One visible constraint is enough to move the system forward if the measurement is trustworthy.
panels.note: Small fixes are fine when measurement is sharp.

panels.title: Repeat with evidence
panels.summary: Every loop should leave behind a better contract, better tooling, or a falsified hypothesis.

callout.title: Key takeaway
points: The loop matters more than any single hardware bet.
Profiling discipline is transferable across model families.
This family should teach method, not just visuals.
